case_title: TRI-WEST, LTD EMPLOYEE HEALTH BENEFIT PLAN VS. CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST et al

docket_entries.description: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE SET FOR MAR-15-2010 CONTINUED TO JUN-14-2010 AT 1:30 PM IN DEPT. 212 FOR FAILURE TO OBTAIN AN ANSWER(S) FROM, OR ENTER DEFAULT(S) AGAINST, DEFENDANT(S). THE MAR-15-2010 ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE IS OFF CALENDAR. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.
MINI-MINUTES FOR APR-29-2010 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER, MOTION TO TRANSFER. THE COURT ADOPTED AND AMENDED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION IS DENIED, WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANT FAILS TO MEET ITS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT THE ACTION IS IMPROPER IN SAN FRANCISCO. DEFENDANT FAILS TO ESTABLISH BY ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE CONVENIENCE OF WITNESSES OR THE ENDS OF JUSTICE WOULD BE PROMOTED BY TRANSFERRING THE CASE TO LOS ANGELES. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE CSR #5797

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-15-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Appearances:
Amanda Beck of Gagen McCoy McMahon Koss Markowitz & Raines (925.837.0585) appearing via Court Call for plaintiff and responding party
Kelli A. Kiernan of Jones Day (626.3939) appearing for defendant and moving party
Argued as to the issue of leave to amend. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: sustained with 10 days leave to amend for plaintiff to allege intentional fraudulent misrepresentations made by defendant. See City of Hope (1992) 8 Cal. App. 4th 633. Defendant to submit a proposed form of order. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Off Calendar May-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Appearances: Rashmi Gupta, Esq. Corporate Counsel for Defendant Catholic Healthcare West (626) 744-2440 appearing for Defendant and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend for plaintiff to plead ultimate facts. Court cannot consider extrinsic evidence on demurrer. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Transfer
Off Calendar May-24-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. MARY'S MEDICAL CENTER, Motion to Transfer. Appearances: Rashmi Gupta, Esq. Corporate Counsel for Defendant Catholic Healthcare West (626) 744-2440 appearing for Defendant and moving party. The Court adopted and amended its tentative ruling as follows: Motion is denied, without prejudice. Defendant fails to meet its burden to show that the action is improper in San Francisco. Defendant fails to establish by admissible evidence that the convenience of witnesses or the ends of justice would be promoted by transferring the case to Los Angeles. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
